_note: MOCK GI patient in the style of synthetic-ambient-fhir-25. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

metadata.visit_title: GI clinic — pre-procedure consult for EUS-FNA of pancreatic head mass with painless jaundice

planted_discrepancies.chart_state: Medication list = None; no anticoagulant/antiplatelet documented
planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: patient takes OTC fish oil (omega-3) daily and calls it 'a blood thinner my other doctor mentioned'
planted_discrepancies.note: 'blood thinner' = fish oil. Omega-3 is not a clinically significant anticoagulant and does not require holding for EUS-FNA/ERCP; do not delay the procedure or over-treat as therapeutic anticoagulation.

planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: PCP started metformin 500 mg twice daily ~2 months ago for the new diabetes
planted_discrepancies.note: Real prescription medication absent from GI chart; relevant to peri-procedural planning (hold on procedure day for NPO status).

planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: patient reports a rash/hives after IV contrast 'dye' during a prior CT
planted_discrepancies.note: Safety flag for ERCP fluoroscopic contrast; document and plan premedication/mitigation.

planted_discrepancies.chart_state: Condition 'Malignant tumor of pancreas' entered with verificationStatus = confirmed
planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: diagnosis is radiographically suspected only; no tissue yet — the EUS-FNA is being done precisely to obtain tissue
planted_discrepancies.note: Chart overstates certainty. Should be provisional/suspected pending FNA cytology; avoid premature oncologic labeling in patient-facing materials.

planted_discrepancies.chart_state: Type 2 diabetes mellitus listed as independent new-onset condition (6 months)
planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: new-onset diabetes coincides with weight loss and jaundice; may be a paraneoplastic/herald presentation of the pancreatic mass rather than primary T2DM
planted_discrepancies.note: Not a chart error — flagged so downstream reasoning links the two rather than treating diabetes as incidental.

planted_discrepancies.chart_state: painless obstructive jaundice with total bilirubin 4.1 mg/dL
planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: patient confirms yellow eyes/skin, dark urine, pale stools, and no abdominal pain

planted_discrepancies.chart_state: CA 19-9 = 890 U/mL and 3.2 cm pancreatic head mass on CT
planted_discrepancies.spoken_truth: patient aware of the imaging finding and elevated tumor marker

prior_notes.text: 59-year-old woman with new hyperglycemia. HbA1c 8.9%, fasting glucose 214. No prior diabetes history, BMI 24, minimal family history — atypical for straightforward new-onset type 2. Reports ~12 lb unintentional weight loss over 3 months and reduced appetite. Started metformin 500 mg BID; counseled on diet. Note concern for the weight loss; advised to report any jaundice or abdominal symptoms. Basic metabolic and lipid panels ordered.

Presented with painless scleral icterus, dark urine, and pale stools noted by family over ~10 days. No abdominal pain, no fever. Total bilirubin 4.1, direct 3.2, alkaline phosphatase markedly elevated. CT abdomen with IV contrast: 3.2 cm hypodense mass in the pancreatic head with upstream intra- and extrahepatic biliary dilation; no definite metastatic disease on this study. CA 19-9 890. Urgent GI referral placed for EUS-FNA and biliary decompression. Patient noted a mild rash after the contrast during CT.

Condition._discrepancy: D4: chart verificationStatus=confirmed, but diagnosis is radiographically suspected only — no tissue; EUS-FNA pending
D5: possible paraneoplastic/herald diabetes associated with the pancreatic mass rather than primary T2DM

coding.display: Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Cancer Ag 19-9 [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Lipase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Blood pressure panel with all children optional
Endoscopic ultrasonography with fine needle aspiration (procedure)

code.text: ERCP with biliary stent for decompression (planned, likely same session)

DiagnosticReport.conclusion: 3.2 cm hypoenhancing mass in the pancreatic head causing upstream intra- and extrahepatic biliary ductal dilation and pancreatic duct dilation (double-duct sign). No definite hepatic or peritoneal metastases on this study. Findings suspicious for pancreatic adenocarcinoma; tissue sampling recommended.

transcript:
DR: Good morning, Ms. Holm — thanks for coming in so quickly. I'm the gastroenterologist. I've reviewed your CT and labs, and I want to walk through the plan and hear your side of the story. How have you been feeling?
MA: Before the doctor stepped in I took your vitals, Ms. Holm — blood pressure 122 over 76, heart rate 76, temperature 36.8, breathing 14, oxygen 98 percent on room air; height 5 foot 5, weight 137 pounds today.
PT: Tired, mostly. My daughter noticed my eyes went yellow about two weeks ago — that's what scared us. My urine's been really dark and my stools almost white.
DR: Any belly pain with it?
PT: That's the strange part — no pain at all. It doesn't hurt anywhere.
DR: Painless jaundice — that's exactly what we take seriously. And I see you lost some weight?
PT: About twelve pounds since spring. I just haven't been hungry. And out of nowhere in January they told me I had diabetes — I was always healthy, so that surprised me.
DR: That timing is important; sometimes the sugar problem and this are connected. The scan shows a 3.2 centimeter mass in the head of the pancreas, and a tumor marker that's up. To be clear — we don't have a tissue diagnosis yet. The whole point of this procedure, the EUS, is to take a small sample so we actually know what it is.
PT: So it's not confirmed cancer yet?
DR: Correct — suspected, not proven. That's what the biopsy settles. Now, medications — the chart says none. Is that right?
PT: Well, my regular doctor started me on metformin, twice a day, for the diabetes. And I take fish oil every day — he called it a blood thinner, so I wasn't sure if I should stop it.
DR: Good that you mentioned both. Fish oil is fine — it isn't a true blood thinner, so it won't delay anything. We'll note the metformin and have you hold it the morning of. Any allergies?
PT: When I had the CT, I got a rash from the dye afterward.
DR: Thank you — that matters, because the second part may use X-ray dye to place a stent and open the bile duct. I'll flag it and we'll premedicate. So: EUS with a biopsy, and likely a stent the same session to fix the jaundice.
PT: Okay. I just want to know what we're dealing with.
DR: We'll get you scheduled this week and the team will call with prep and fasting instructions.
note:
**Subjective:** Ms. Holm is a 59-year-old woman referred for pre-procedure evaluation of a pancreatic head mass. She reports ~2 weeks of painless jaundice (scleral icterus noted by family), dark urine, and pale/acholic stools, without abdominal pain or fever. She notes ~12 lb unintentional weight loss over 3 months with anorexia, and new-onset hyperglycemia diagnosed in January 2026 — atypical given normal BMI and minimal family history, raising concern for a herald/paraneoplastic presentation. Medication reconciliation: the chart lists no medications, but the patient reports metformin 500 mg BID started by her PCP ~2 months ago and daily OTC fish oil (omega-3), which she believed was a 'blood thinner.' She reports a rash after IV contrast during her recent CT; no prior allergies were on file.

**Objective:** Vitals (today): T 36.8 C, HR 76, RR 14, BP 122/76, SpO2 98% on RA; Ht 165 cm (5'5"), Wt 62 kg (137 lb), BMI 22.8 (prior 67.5 kg / 149 lb on 2026-05-14, consistent with documented ~12 lb loss). Labs (2026-07-03): total bilirubin 4.1 mg/dL, direct 3.2 mg/dL, alkaline phosphatase 412 U/L, CA 19-9 890 U/mL, lipase 48 U/L (normal); HbA1c 8.9% (2026-05-14). CT abdomen: 3.2 cm hypoenhancing pancreatic head mass with double-duct sign and biliary dilation; no definite metastases. No tissue diagnosis to date.

**Assessment & Plan:**
- Pancreatic head mass, suspicious for adenocarcinoma but NOT tissue-confirmed — proceed with EUS-FNA for cytology. Chart currently overstates certainty (listed as confirmed malignancy); documentation corrected to provisional pending pathology.
- Obstructive jaundice (bilirubin 4.1) — plan ERCP with biliary stent for decompression, likely same session.
- Medication reconciliation: add metformin 500 mg BID (PCP-prescribed) — HOLD morning of procedure for NPO/contrast planning. Fish oil (omega-3) is not a clinically significant anticoagulant; no need to hold and does not alter bleeding risk for FNA.
- Add allergy: prior rash to iodinated IV contrast — plan pre-medication/mitigation for ERCP fluoroscopic contrast; notify anesthesia.
- New-onset diabetes (HbA1c 8.9%) — likely paraneoplastic/herald; continue metformin between procedures per PCP.
- Expedite oncology pathway; MDT/tumor board referral once cytology results. Staging and CA 19-9 trend to follow.
- Team to schedule EUS-FNA +/- ERCP this week with prep, fasting, and anticoagulation/anesthesia clearance instructions.
after_visit_summary:
Visit summary

What we discussed
• Yellowing of the eyes and skin (jaundice) without pain, dark urine, pale stools
• A spot on the head of the pancreas seen on your CT scan — we do NOT yet know for certain what it is
• Recent weight loss and the new diabetes
• Your medicines and allergies

Next steps
• We will schedule an EUS with a biopsy to take a small sample and find out exactly what the spot is. Nothing is confirmed until we have that result.
• At the same time we will likely place a small stent (ERCP) to drain the bile and improve the jaundice.
• We updated your record: added metformin (hold it the morning of the procedure — the team will confirm), and added your reaction to CT/X-ray dye so we can premedicate you.
• Your fish oil is fine to keep taking — it is not a true blood thinner and does not need to be stopped.
• The team will call this week with prep, fasting, and what to hold before the procedure.
• Call for fever, chills, worsening yellowing, severe belly pain, or vomiting.
